message:
feat(dashboard): add granular permission management for viewers and editors

Implements fine-grained dashboard sharing with separate viewer and editor
permission lists, enabling team collaboration with proper access control.

dashboard.service.ts:
- Add splitPermissions() to separate viewer/editor lists from DB rows
- Add normalizeUserList() to dedupe and trim permission user IDs
- Add syncDashboardPermissions() for atomic permission batch updates
  - Uses upsert with conflict handling for efficient updates
  - Supports partial updates (viewers only, editors only, or both)
  - Properly removes users no longer in the permission list
- Add updateSharing() public API for modifying dashboard permissions
- Integrate permission sync into createDashboard and updateDashboard
- Load permission relationships in getDashboard and listDashboards

dashboard.service.test.ts:
- Add test for persisting viewers/editors on create
- Add tests for updateSharing with partial updates
- Add test for full viewer/editor replacement
- Verify permission isolation (updating viewers doesn't affect editors)

checkpoint.test.ts:
- Update tests to use new permission structure

The implementation ensures:
- Editors get full edit access, viewers get read-only
- User IDs are deduplicated and trimmed
- Permissions are tracked with grantedBy and grantedAt metadata
- Atomic updates prevent race conditions

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
